reference.title: The differences in interface renaming with profiles in ifcfg and keyfile format

section.p: You can define custom network interface names, such as lan to make interface names more descriptive. In this case, the udev service renames the interfaces. The renaming process works differently depending on whether you use connection profiles in ifcfg or keyfile format.

dlentry.dt: The interface renaming process when using a profile in ifcfg format

li.p: The udev rule file /usr/lib/udev/rules.d/60-net.rules calls the /lib/udev/rename_device helper utility.
The helper utility searches for the HWADDR parameter in /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-* files.
If the value set in the variable matches the MAC address of an interface, the helper utility renames the interface to the name set in the DEVICE parameter of the file.

dlentry.dt: The interface renaming process when using a profile in keyfile format

li.p: Create a systemd link file or a udev rule to rename an interface.
Use the custom interface name in the interface-name property of a NetworkManager connection profile.

p.xref: How the udev device manager renames network interfaces
Configuring user-defined network interface names by using udev rules
Configuring user-defined network interface names by using systemd link files
